qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Groovy code injection via unsanitized matrix exclude filter evaluated by GroovyShell.evaluate()
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: While this finding targets the correct Jervis repository, it identifies a different vulnerability in different code. The finding describes unsanitized string interpolation into Groovy expressions in MultiPlatformGenerator.groovy:526 and LifecycleGenerator.groovy:571 (code injection via matrix exclude filter). The advisory describes a deterministic PBKDF2 salt derived from sha256Sum(passphrase) in SecurityIO.groovy:869-870,894-895. The vulnerable functions, files, root causes, and vulnerability classes are entirely different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability: deterministic PBKDF2 salt derived from sha256Sum(passphrase) in SecurityIO.groovy of the Jervis library. The 'groovy-injection-matrix-exclude' finding targets the correct Jervis repository but describes a completely different vulnerability (Groovy code injection via matrix exclude filter) in different source files (MultiPlatformGenerator.groovy, LifecycleGenerator.groovy) with a different root cause (unsanitized string interpolation vs. deterministic salt generation).
evidence: Advisory references SecurityIO.groovy:869-870 and SecurityIO.groovy:894-895 where salt = sha256Sum(passphrase).
No finding references SecurityIO.groovy or any PBKDF2/key derivation code path.
The 'groovy-injection-matrix-exclude' finding references MultiPlatformGenerator.groovy:526 and LifecycleGenerator.groovy:571 — different files, different functions, different root cause.
